title: US Counterterrorism Aircraft Could Be Surprisingly Useful in a Taiwan War
gist: The US military is reorienting from counterinsurgency toward a peer fight with China, where current capabilities would be inadequate, yet some Global War on Terror aircraft — MALE drones, subsonic ground-attack planes, and attack helicopters — could be adapted to new roles in a Taiwan conflict. (Text is a truncated teaser ending in '[...]'.)

themes.theme: Pivot from GWOT to peer conflict

insights.claim: The US military is preparing for conflict with China that would differ in scale and kind from recent wars, and the author argues current US capabilities would be inadequate for such a fight.
insights.speaker: Alex Lee
insights.so_what: An explicit capability gap versus China underpins sustained US defense-modernization spending — a structural defense-budget tailwind.
insights.quote: The U.S. military's current capabilities would be inadequate.

themes.theme: Repurposing GWOT aircraft for the Pacific

insights.claim: Three GWOT-era aircraft types — medium-altitude long-endurance (MALE) drones, subsonic ground-attack planes, and attack helicopters — could be adapted for new roles in a Taiwan conflict despite struggling in their traditional roles in contested airspace.
insights.speaker: Alex Lee
insights.so_what: Repurposing legacy platforms suggests value in existing inventory and upgrade programs rather than only new-build systems — relevant to defense-prime sustainment revenue.
insights.quote: Three types of aircraft would struggle to serve their traditional roles in contested airspace, but can be adapted for new ones

insights.claim: Aircraft known for striking from short distances and lacking speed and stealth would pose severe risks if used against a modern adversary like China in contested airspace.
insights.speaker: Alex Lee
insights.so_what: Survivability limits of legacy platforms in contested airspace point to demand for stealth, standoff, and unmanned attritable systems.
insights.quote: using them against a modern adversary would pose severe risks
